featured.title: Deutsches Technikmuseum Berlin: The Giant Museum With a Plane on the Roof
featured.summary: A full-size C-47 sits on a roof in Kreuzberg, and under it waits Berlin’s biggest museum complex: 40+ aircraft, two real 1874 roundhouses, a giant ship hall and a hands-on science centre on one ticket. Here is how to plan it without losing a day.
featured.alt: The Douglas C-47 Rosinenbomber mounted on the roof of the Deutsches Technikmuseum's glass New Building
featured.label: New: museum day

miniPosts.title: Köpenick Berlin: The Palace, the Lake and the Fake Captain Who Fooled Prussia
miniPosts.summary: Berlin’s oldest corner is an island town in the far southeast: a Baroque palace, a fishermen’s lane, the city’s biggest lake and the town hall a fake captain robbed in 1906. Here is how to plan the half day by S-Bahn, on one AB ticket.
miniPosts.alt: View across the Frauentog bay to the Köpenick old town island with its church and town hall towers

miniPosts.title: Karl-Marx-Allee: How to Walk East Berlin's Showpiece Boulevard
miniPosts.summary: Karl-Marx-Allee is East Berlin's 2.3 km showpiece boulevard, and most visitors never see it. Here is the stretch worth walking, the facade details to look for, the 1953 uprising that started here, and what is actually open, from the reopened Kino International to Café Sibylle.
miniPosts.alt: The twin towers of Frankfurter Tor with Karl-Marx-Allee running between them under a blue sky

miniPosts.title: Leaving Berlin by Train: Which Station, Which Ticket, and When the Slow Train Wins
miniPosts.summary: Zoologischer Garten and Ostbahnhof have no long-distance stop until 12 December 2026, and at Hauptbahnhof you now need the lower level rather than the glass hall upstairs. Here is which Berlin station your train actually leaves from, how Super Sparpreis, Sparpreis and Flexpreis really differ, and the Deutschlandticket shortcut that quietly stopped working in December 2025.
miniPosts.alt: The glass barrel-vault roof over the upper cross-city platforms at Berlin Hauptbahnhof, with tracks curving away and travellers on the platform

miniPosts.title: Tropical Islands from Berlin: A Rainforest Day Trip Inside a Giant Airship Hangar
miniPosts.summary: An hour south of Berlin, one of the world’s largest free-standing halls holds a 26 degree beach, a real rainforest and a 27 metre slide tower. Here is how the trains work in 2026, what your group actually pays, and when the dome beats the city.
miniPosts.alt: Aerial view of the Tropical Islands dome standing alone in the Brandenburg landscape

posts.title: Spandau Berlin: The Old Town Berlin Is Not Supposed to Have
posts.summary: Berlin is supposed to have no old town, but Spandau never got that memo. The district is five years older than Berlin on paper, keeps a full-height stretch of 14th-century town wall, and guards a complete moated Renaissance fortress with the oldest building in the city inside. Here is what actually deserves your time, in what order, and how the 2026 rail works change getting there.
posts.alt: Aerial view of Spandau Citadel showing the complete star-shaped Renaissance fortress surrounded by its moat, with the Juliusturm tower rising at one corner

posts.title: The Berlin Wall Trail: Which Section Is Actually Worth Your Time
posts.summary: The Berliner Mauerweg rings the whole of former West Berlin, so about 50 km runs through the city and roughly 110 km is forest, lake and farmland. The three sections with the highest concentration of surviving Wall and border sites total about 19 km. Here is how to pick a section by what is still standing on it, not by what looks convenient on a map.
posts.alt: Preserved section of the Berlin Wall at the Bernauer Strasse memorial, running into the distance with rusted steel posts continuing the line

posts.title: Is Berlin Walkable? How Far Apart the Main Sights Really Are
posts.summary: Is Berlin walkable? The central sights are far closer than the map suggests, and flat underfoot. Here is how far apart they really are, the one walk to do on foot, and when to take the train instead.
posts.alt: The Bode Museum dome and the TV Tower rising across the Spree on Berlin's Museum Island, two of the central sights that sit within an easy walk of each other
